name: Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop
name_original: Sierra de Laguarres – circular desde Benabarre
description: Mountain bike the moderate 23.2-mile Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop, featuring varied terrain and views of castles and reservoirs.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Benabarre offers diverse terrain at the gates of the Pyrenees, characterized by the Sierra de Laguarres, Sierra de la Carrodilla, and El Montsec de l'Estall. The region features significant karst lakes, including the Estaña Lakes, and dramatic geological formations like the Ciscar Gorge and the "Chinese Wall of Finestres." Trails traverse varied landscapes, from agricultural land and oak forests to rocky, steep paths, providing a range of challenges for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Benabarre The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and offers views of castles and reservoirs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Finestras Abandoned Village – Chinese Wall of Finestres loop from Estopiñán del Castillo / Estopanyà, a moderate 16.8 miles (27.1 km) path. This route combines historical exploration with natural beauty, including the impressive "Chinese Wall of Finestres." Local mountain bikers also love the Penavera Bridge – Finestras Abandoned Village loop from Estopiñán del Castillo / Estopanyà, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail leading through varied natural trails connecting villages and pastures, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Benabarre is defined by Pyrenean views, karst lakes, and varied terrain including oak forests and rocky paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more advanced routes. The routes in Benabarre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Benabarre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Benabarre?
A: There are over 75 mountain bike trails in the Benabarre region, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Benabarre?
A: The trails around Benabarre cater to a range of abilities. You'll find approximately 6 easy routes, 55 moderate routes, and 14 more challenging routes for experienced riders. The terrain varies from agricultural land and oak forests to rocky, steep paths.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Benabarre?
A: Benabarre offers diverse and striking natural landscapes. You can expect breathtaking Pyrenean views, significant karst lakes like the Estaña Lakes, and dramatic geological formations such as the Ciscar Gorge and the impressive "Chinese Wall of Finestres." Trails often traverse varied terrain, including oak forests and demanding ascents through rocky paths.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Benabarre?
A: Yes, many routes in Benabarre are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977243320/sierra-del-castillo-de-laguarres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.2-mile ride featuring varied terrain and views of castles and reservoirs. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerillo Reservoir – Hermitage of San Medardo loop from Benabarre / Benavarri&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter, moderate ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails pass by or offer views of significant landmarks. You might encounter historical sites like the Benabarre Castle, perched on a limestone platform, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village&lt;/a&gt;. Natural wonders include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2670454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinese Wall of Finestres&lt;/a&gt; and the Estaña Lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/323964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montfalcó Walkways&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/494069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible nearby for those looking for more adventure.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Benabarre?
A: The mountain bike routes in Benabarre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning Pyrenean views, and the unique blend of natural beauty and historical sites found along the trails.

Q: Are there any routes that combine historical exploration with natural beauty?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village – Chinese Wall of Finestres loop from Estopiñán del Castillo / Estopanyà&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.8-mile path that perfectly blends historical exploration with the impressive natural beauty of the "Chinese Wall of Finestres."

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Benabarre?
A: Benabarre is situated at the gates of the Pyrenees, so spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winter might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Many routes provide excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Benabarre – Torres del Obispo loop from Benabarre / Benavarri&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, especially around the Benabarre Castle. Additionally, routes passing through areas like the Estaña Lakes often provide scenic vistas, such as the views from the hermitage of the Virgen de Terrers.

Q: Are there any amenities like picnic areas or playgrounds along the trails?
A: Yes, the Hermitage of San Medardo, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerillo Reservoir – Hermitage of San Medardo loop from Benabarre / Benavarri&lt;/a&gt;, features a park with amenities like a playground, picnic area, and barbecues, making it an ideal spot for a relaxing stop during or after your ride.

Q: What kind of varied natural trails can I expect to ride on?
A: The region's trails are often hundreds or thousands of years old, connecting villages, mines, and pastures. You'll ride through diverse landscapes, including agricultural land, dense oak forests, and challenging rocky, steep paths, providing a ride rich in both nature and history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penavera Bridge – Finestras Abandoned Village loop from Estopiñán del Castillo / Estopanyà&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a route leading through such varied natural trails.
tips:
Access point to the Castle and Church (in the image) by stairs.

Numerous battles were fought in the castle of Benabarre and it was demolished at the beginning of the 17th century and reused in the 19th century with new executions, remaining active in the Catalan and Carlist wars. Due to these war conflicts, it was rebuilt to transform it into a rifle fort.

Today it looks more like a 19th century fort, although it retains older walls from when it was the castle-palace of the counts of Ribagorza. Thus, it is a monumental complex of a single enclosure where all the transformations of the property parallel to the historical ups and downs are integrated.

It is located at the top of the town and is visible from several points in the area. It is made up of the Romanesque church and the fortress, dating from the 12th century. It consists of two enclosures, an upper one, older and with a Muslim layout, of which there are walls at the base of the rock on which it sits, as well as the base of a quadrangular tower.
The Cerillo reservoir is in the Barranco de San Medardo, in an area crossed by the GR 18.1 and also known as the Vereda de los Pinares.

Castarlenas is a place that is well worth a stop and a visit if you pedal by. An ideal place to catch your breath while enjoying the views and observing the remains of the buildings of this town that has been uninhabited for half a century.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop is rated as moderate. This means you should expect a ride that can extend up to 5 hours with up to 758 meters (2,487 feet) of elevation gain. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills, correlating to STS S2 difficulty. The terrain can be varied and sometimes technical, with chunky sections, rocks, and loose gravel.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse and often technical terrain on this loop. Expect 'chunky' sections with rocks and rough paths, along with steep climbs and descents. Surfaces can be loose and covered in thick gravel, sometimes requiring you to 'surf' with your bike. The route passes through singletrack and wider paths, shaped by the region's limestone massifs.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop typically takes around 3 hours and 31 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level, skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or points of interest.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near Benabarre. You should be able to find suitable parking in or around Benabarre to begin your ride. Specific parking areas for mountain bikers are usually well-marked in the town.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop is located in the La Ribagorza region. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on the public trails in this area. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations before your ride, especially if you plan to deviate from the main route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: As you ride, you'll pass several notable points of interest. These include the historic Castillo de Benabarre, the serene Cerillo Reservoir, and the intriguing Ruins of Castarlenas. These spots offer great opportunities for a break and some photos.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other mountain biking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop incorporates sections of several other trails in the region. You'll find yourself on parts of 'La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 3. Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres', 'La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 5. Ruta de los Mases', and 'Senderos de La Ribagorza', among others. This integration offers a rich and varied riding experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop?
faqs.answer: The Huesca province, where this loop is located, benefits from a generally friendly and sunny winter climate. This allows for riding at moderate altitudes without extreme cold or snow. Therefore, late autumn, winter, and early spring are often ideal, as summer can be quite hot.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a moderate mountain bike ride and bring plenty of water for both of you.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the starting point of Benabarre offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the town, making it a convenient base for your mountain biking adventure in the Sierra del Castillo de Laguarres region.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I encounter on the ride?
faqs.answer: Prepare for an incredible variety of landscapes. The route offers panoramic views of the Sierra, passing through beautiful forests, cultivated fields, and dramatic rock formations. You'll also encounter historical elements like castles and ruins, adding to the visual interest of your ride.
